content.text:
Perfect! Here are all six files with their complete content: --- ## AddBookModal.tsx ```typescript 'use client' import { useState } from 'react' import Modal from './Modal' interface Props { onClose: () => void onSuccess: () => void } export default function AddBookModal({ onClose, onSuccess }: Props) { const [form, setForm] = useState({ title: '', total_pages: '', current_page: '0' }) const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false) const [error, setError] = useState('') async function handleSubmit(e: React.FormEvent) { e.preventDefault() setError('') setLoading(true) const res = await fetch('/api/books', { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, body: JSON.stringify({ title: form.title.trim(), total_pages: Number(form.total_pages), current_page: Number(form.current_page), sessions_per_week: 4, }), }) if (!res.ok) { const data = await res.json() setError(data.error ?? 'Failed to add book') setLoading(false) return } onSuccess() onClose() } return (
setForm(f => ({ ...f, title: e.target.value }))} className="w-full border border-parchment-300 bg-parchment-50 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 text-sm text-parchment-900 placeholder:text-parchment-400 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-forest-700/20 focus:border-forest-600 transition-colors" placeholder="Book title" />
setForm(f => ({ ...f, total_pages: e.target.value }))} className="w-full border border-parchment-300 bg-parchment-50 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 text-sm text-parchment-900 placeholder:text-parchment-400 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-forest-700/20 focus:border-forest-600 transition-colors" placeholder="450" />
setForm(f => ({ ...f, current_page: e.target.value }))} className="w-full border border-parchment-300 bg-parchment-50 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 text-sm text-parchment-900 placeholder:text-parchment-400 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-forest-700/20 focus:border-forest-600 transition-colors" placeholder="0" />
{error &&

{error}

}
) } ``` --- ## EditBookModal.tsx ```typescript 'use client' import { useState } from 'react' import Modal from './Modal' import type { Book } from '@/types' interface Props { book: Book onClose: () => void onSuccess: () => void } export default function EditBookModal({ book, onClose, onSuccess }: Props) { const [form, setForm] = useState({ title: book.title, total_pages: String(book.total_pages), current_page: String(book.current_page), }) const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false) const [error, setError] = useState('') async function handleSubmit(e: React.FormEvent) { e.preventDefault() setError('') setLoading(true) const res = await fetch(`/api/books/${book.id}`, { method: 'PATCH', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, body: JSON.stringify({ title: form.title.trim(), total_pages: Number(form.total_pages), current_page: Number(form.current_page), }), }) if (!res.ok) { const data = await res.json() setError(data.error ?? 'Failed to update book') setLoading(false) return } onSuccess() onClose() } return (
setForm(f => ({ ...f, title: e.target.value }))} className="w-full border border-parchment-300 bg-parchment-50 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 text-sm text-parchment-900 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-forest-700/20 focus:border-forest-600 transition-colors" />
setForm(f => ({ ...f, total_pages: e.target.value }))} className="w-full border border-parchment-300 bg-parchment-50 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 text-sm text-parchment-900 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-forest-700/20 focus:border-forest-600 transition-colors" />
setForm(f => ({ ...f, current_page: e.target.value }))} className="w-full border border-parchment-300 bg-parchment-50 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 text-sm text-parchment-900 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-forest-700/20 focus:border-forest-600 transition-colors" />
{error &&

}
) } ``` --- ## AddArticleModal.tsx ```typescript 'use client' import { useState } from 'react' interface Props { onClose: () => void onSuccess: () => void } export default function AddArticleModal({ onClose, onSuccess }: Props) { const [title, setTitle] = useState('') const [submitting, setSubmitting] = useState(false) const [error, setError] = useState('') async function handleSubmit(e: React.FormEvent) { e.preventDefault() if (!title.trim()) return setSubmitting(true) setError('') const res = await fetch('/api/books', { method: 'POST', headers: { 'Content-Type': 'application/json' }, body: JSON.stringify({ title: title.trim(), type: 'article' }), }) if (!res.ok) { const data = await res.json() setError(data.error ?? 'Something went wrong') setSubmitting(false) return } onSuccess() onClose() } return (

'Failed to complete session') setLoading(false) return } onSuccess() onClose() } return (

{session.book.title}

Planned: pp. {session.pages_start}–{session.pages_end}

setActualPage(e.target.value)} className="w-full border border-parchment-300 bg-parchment-50 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 text-sm text-parchment-900 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-forest-700/20 focus:border-forest-600 transition-colors" />

Defaults to planned end page. Adjust if you read more or less.

{error &&

{error}

}
) } ``` --- ## EditSessionModal.tsx ```typescript 'use client' import { useState } from 'react' import Modal from './Modal' import { supabase } from '@/lib/supabase' import type { Session, Book } from '@/types' interface Props { session: Session & { book: Book } onClose: () => void onSuccess: () => void } export default function EditSessionModal({ session, onClose, onSuccess }: Props) { const [date, setDate] = useState(session.scheduled_date) const [pagesEnd, setPagesEnd] = useState(String(session.pages_end)) const [loading, setLoading] = useState(false) const [error, setError] = useState('') async function handleSubmit(e: React.FormEvent) { e.preventDefault() setError('') setLoading(true) const { error: err } = await supabase .from('sessions') .update({ scheduled_date: date, pages_end: Number(pagesEnd) }) .eq('id', session.id) if (err) { setError(err.message) setLoading(false) return } onSuccess() onClose() } return (

{session.book.title}

pp. {session.pages_start}–{session.pages_end}

setDate(e.target.value)} className="w-full border border-parchment-300 bg-parchment-50 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 text-sm text-parchment-900 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-forest-700/20 focus:border-forest-600 transition-colors" />
setPagesEnd(e.target.value)} className="w-full border border-parchment-300 bg-parchment-50 rounded-lg px-3 py-2 text-sm text-parchment-900 focus:outline-none focus:ring-2 focus:ring-forest-700/20 focus:border-forest-600 transition-colors" />
{error &&

{error}

}
) } ``` --- ## SessionDetailModal.tsx ```typescript 'use client' import { format, parseISO } from 'date-fns' import Modal from './Modal' import type { Book, Session } from '@/types' interface Props { session: Session & { book: Book } onClose: () => void onComplete: () => void onEdit: () => void } export default function SessionDetailModal({ session, onClose, onComplete, onEdit }: Props) { const pageCount = session.pages_end - session.pages_start + 1 const pctAfter = session.book.total_pages > 0 ? Math.round((session.pages_end / session.book.total_pages) * 100) : 0 return (

{session.book.title}

{format(parseISO(session.scheduled_date), 'EEEE, MMMM d')}

pp. {session.pages_start}–{session.pages_end} · {pageCount} pages · {pctAfter}% of book

) } ``` --- All six files are now complete with full content. They're all client-side React components using TypeScript, styled with Tailwind CSS using the parchment and forest color scales from your design system. Most modals use the shared Modal wrapper component, except AddArticleModal which implements its own modal structure.
